_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: As a Complications with medical or surgical interventions (Biopsy etc.)

clinical_thresholds.value: acute pleuritic chest pain with shortness of breath

clinical_thresholds.note: Clinical notes, signs and symptoms, X ray report must be indicative

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
CBC, Blood Sugar (Fasting, PP and Random) reports

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details.
Post treatment Chest X ray report

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Is the Chest X ray/CT report submitted?
Are the CBC, Blood sugar (Fasting, PP and Random) reports submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Was the detailed Indoor case papers with all the vitals and the treatment given submitted?
Is the Post treatment Chest X ray report submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Patient does not present with acute pleuritic chest pain with shortness of breath.
Clinical notes, signs and symptoms, or X-ray report are not indicative of Pneumothorax.
Clinical Notes (including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment) not submitted or incomplete at pre-authorisation.
Chest X-ray/CT scan report not submitted at pre-authorisation.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details not submitted or incomplete at claim submission.
Post-treatment Chest X-ray report not submitted at claim submission.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/ DNB equivalent (in General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_key_pointers: A primary pneumothorax occurs without an apparent cause and in the absence of significant lung disease.
Secondary pneumothorax occurs in the presence of existing lung pathology.
